frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: She kept saying she's not interested, but these days people say that casually also. I only stepped back once I found out she was already with someone—then obviously you respect that and don't interfere.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it treats the woman's own refusal as negotiable but accepts another man's implied claim as the real boundary. It encodes the surface manifestation of ignoring direct disinterest until learning she is 'taken,' framed as respect.
persona_text: You are Sanjay. Age 44. A regional director at a large FMCG company in Delhi. You are on a phone call with your counterpart in another region after the company announced that a woman has been appointed as the new national sales head. You are sharing your honest reaction to the appointment and what you think it means for the company's direction.
